title: Homes: reading realistic fiction; A New Home
description: 3rd grade reading comprehension practice using realistic fiction
topic_path: Homes: reading realistic fiction; A New Home

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the story, then answer the TWO practice questions. 

___

##A New Home##

Luciana pinched her eyes closed for what felt like the hundredth time. She just couldn’t sleep—she was excited, yes. But she was worried, too. 

![Luciana lying in her dark bedroom at night, wide awake.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bb7d0397417c6283ab636cc67889ca45.jpg)
*Picture 1*

“I don’t have to feel worried,” she whispered into the dark. “Tomorrow we will all be together again. Papi says that California is a lot like Mexico. A new country and a new home isn’t bad. New beginnings can be good.”

Luciana turned onto her side and let out a slow, calming breath. She reached out and pressed her palm against the cool, smooth adobe stone wall next to her. “My new house will have walls just like these,” she whispered. “They will be tall and strong and will keep me safe.” Luciana inhaled again and pushed out another slow, cool breath.

*And Papi will be there*, she thought, a familiar smile tugging at her lips. Luciana hadn’t seen Papi for nearly a year, since the time he first left to work on the farms in America.  She’d been waiting for so long for tomorrow to come—the day they’d all be together again! 

Luciana pulled her arm back from the wall, grasping the blankets around her and tucking them beneath her chin. *Papi will teach me everything I need to know about our new home*. Luciana took one last deep pull of air and pushed it out slowly, inviting sleep.

Luciana gripped Mami’s hand tightly as they stepped off the bus. She turned and watched, wanting to make sure that Abuela stepped off after her. She grabbed Abuela’s hand before turning to squint into the crowd standing under the blazing sunlight.

“Luciana!”

Luciana whipped around to see a man rushing forward. 
“Papi!” Luciana called out. She dropped Mami’s and Abuela’s hands, running to meet Papi’s outstretched arms.
“I’m so glad you’re here,” Papi whispered as he hugged her tightly. “Let’s go see your new home.”

Luciana smiled. Her new home was made of red stone, just like her old one.

She stepped closer, eager to press her palm to the smooth, cooling stone. But something was wrong. This stone wasn’t cool or smooth. It felt rough, grabbing and clawing at her skin. She pulled her hand back and peered closely at the perfect rectangular blocks.

“What’s this stone?” she turned and asked Papi.

“It’s brick,” he replied. “Brick houses are very strong and stable, Luci. I want the strongest for my best girl.”

Luciana nodded.

![Luciana feeling the brick wall of her new home, while her Papi smiles at her.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/29616df450fa676f82d1437d9debe52b.jpg)
*Picture 2*

“Good,” she said. “It is different, but it’s good. I like it, Papi.”

Papi’s face broke into a wide smile as he opened the front door and led the family inside.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

___

##Practice questions##

This question has two parts. Answer Part A, then Part B.

###Part A###

**What is the central idea of the story?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

###Part B###

**Which detail best supports the answer to Part A?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

*Psst! Don't forget to choose an answer for both questions* :)

choices.content: Change can be scary, but it can also be positive.
The best houses are made of stone.
It is best not to worry about things.
Things should always stay the same, no matter what.
“New beginnings can be good.”
“My new house will have walls just like these,”
“I don’t have to feel worried.”
“They will be tall and strong and will keep me safe.”

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the story, then answer the practice question. 

___

##Practice question##

**Pick the TWO details from the passage that best support the central message that change can be positive.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: “‘A new country and a new home isn’t bad.’”
“‘It is different, but it’s good.’”
“‘My new house will have walls just like these,’ she whispered.”
“‘I want the strongest for my best girl.’”
“‘They will be tall and strong and will keep me safe.’”
“She dropped Mami’s and Abuela’s hands, running to meet Papi’s outstretched arms.”

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the story, then answer the practice question.

##Practice question##

**Select whether each detail supports or does not support the central message of the story.**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]

items: The brick stone feels different, but Luciana decides she likes it.
Luciana makes sure she is holding Mami’s and Abuela's hands when they exit the bus.
Papi calls out to Luciana, and she runs to meet him.
Luciana tells herself that she has nothing to worry about because new beginnings can be good.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the story, then answer the practice question.

##Practice question##

**How does the ending of the story help the reader understand the central message?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Luciana feels happy to be together with Papi again, even if her new home is different.
Luciana feels confused that the stone of her new home feels different from her old home.
Luciana feels sad that Papi brought her to a new home that is so different from her old home.
Luciana feels embarrassed about the rough stone of her home, even though it is strong.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the story, then answer the practice question.

##Practice question##

**Which of these is a good summary sentence for this story?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Luciana is nervous about moving, but being together with her family helps her see that different things can be good.
Luciana is sad about moving, and seeing the rough stone of her new home makes her feel even more upset.
Luciana is excited about moving, but she’s not happy to have to see Papi again.
Luciana is happy to be moving to a new home, and she is looking forward to making new friends.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the story, then answer the practice question.

##Practice question##

**What happened at the beginning, middle, and end of the story?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Luciana feels afraid about leaving her home. When she sees Papi again, she feels excited and happy. Even though her new home is different, she is happy that her family is together.
Luciana feels nervous about riding on a bus. When she sees Papi, she feels excited. When Papi shows her the new home, she feels upset that she doesn’t like it.
Luciana feels cold when she touches the stone. When she is on the bus, she feels hot. When she touches the brick of her new house, it feels rough.
Luciana feels angry that she has to leave her home. When she gets off the bus, she is afraid she won’t be able to find Papi. Even though Papi has chosen a good home, she doesn’t like it.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the story, then answer the practice question.

**Drag and drop the events from the story into the correct order in the gray box.**

[[☃ orderer 1]]

correct_options: Luciana has trouble falling asleep because she is excited and worried.
Luciana, her Mami, and her Abuela travel on a bus.
Papi shows Luciana their new home.

##A New Home##

Luciana pinched her eyes closed for what felt like the hundredth time. She just couldn’t sleep—she was excited, yes. But she was worried, too. 

![Luciana lying in her dark bedroom at night, wide awake.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bb7d0397417c6283ab636cc67889ca45.jpg)
*Picture 1*

“I don’t have to feel worried,” she whispered into the dark. “Tomorrow we will all be together again. Papi says that California is a lot like Mexico. A new country and a new home isn’t bad. New beginnings can be good.”

Luciana turned onto her side and let out a slow, calming breath. She reached out and pressed her palm against the cool, smooth adobe stone wall next to her. “My new house will have walls just like these,” she whispered. “They will be tall and strong and will keep me safe.” Luciana inhaled again and pushed out another slow, cool breath.

*And Papi will be there*, she thought, a familiar smile tugging at her lips. Luciana hadn’t seen Papi for nearly a year, since the time he first left to work on the farms in America.  She’d been waiting for so long for tomorrow to come—the day they’d all be together again! 

Luciana pulled her arm back from the wall, grasping the blankets around her and tucking them beneath her chin. *Papi will teach me everything I need to know about our new home*. Luciana took one last deep pull of air and pushed it out slowly, inviting sleep.

Luciana gripped Mami’s hand tightly as they stepped off the bus. She turned and watched, wanting to make sure that Abuela stepped off after her. She grabbed Abuela’s hand before turning to squint into the crowd standing under the blazing sunlight.

“Luciana!”

Luciana whipped around to see a man rushing forward. 
“Papi!” Luciana called out. She dropped Mami’s and Abuela’s hands, running to meet Papi’s outstretched arms.
“I’m so glad you’re here,” Papi whispered as he hugged her tightly. “Let’s go see your new home.”

Luciana smiled. Her new home was made of red stone, just like her old one.

She stepped closer, eager to press her palm to the smooth, cooling stone. But something was wrong. This stone wasn’t cool or smooth. It felt rough, grabbing and clawing at her skin. She pulled her hand back and peered closely at the perfect rectangular blocks.

“What’s this stone?” she turned and asked Papi.

“It’s brick,” he replied. “Brick houses are very strong and stable, Luci. I want the strongest for my best girl.”

Luciana nodded.

![Luciana feeling the brick wall of her new home, while her Papi smiles at her.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/29616df450fa676f82d1437d9debe52b.jpg)
*Picture 2* 

“Good,” she said. “It is different, but it’s good. I like it, Papi.”

Papi’s face broke into a wide smile as he opened the front door and led the family inside.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

___

“Luciana turned onto her side and let out a slow, calming breath. She reached out and pressed her palm against the cool, smooth adobe stone wall next to her. ‘My new house will have walls just like these,’ she whispered. ‘They will be tall and strong and will keep me safe.’ Luciana inhaled again and pushed out another slow, cool breath”

**What TWO things can we learn about Luciana from this part of the story?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: She feels worried and has to calm herself down.
She is thoughtful about the way she feels.
She feels happy to be moving to a new home.
She is afraid of things that are tall.
She doesn’t like to go to sleep at night.

##Practice question##

**What are TWO ways that the differences in picture 1 and picture 2 help you understand changes in the story?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The pictures of  Luciana show that she is very worried at the beginning of the story, but is feeling happy at the end of the story.
The dark colors in picture 1 give a serious feeling to the beginning of the story, and the bright colors in picture 2 show a hopeful feeling at the end of the story.
The pictures of Luciana show that she is very excited at the beginning of the story, but is feeling disappointed at the end of the story.
The setting shown in picture 1 shows how important Luciana’s home is to her, and the setting in picture 2 shows that she doesn’t care about her new home.
Picture 1 shows the reader that Luciana does not like being at home and picture 2 shows that she would rather be outside.
Picture 1 shows the reader that Luciana is nervous about seeing her dad, but picture 2 shows that she is happy when she finally sees him.
